Name: Ledyba
Flavor-Text: These very cowardly Pokémon join together and use Reflect to protect their nest.

Name: Ledian
Effects: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It flies through the night sky, sprinkling sparkly dust. According to some, if that dust sticks to you, good things will happen to you.

Name: Shuckle
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: The berries stored in its vaselike shell eventually become a thick, pulpy juice.

Name: Roselia
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It uses the different poisons in each hand separately when it attacks. The stronger its aroma, the healthier it is.

Name: Roserade
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.
Flavor-Text: The poison in its right hand is quick acting. The poison in its left hand is slow acting. Both are life threatening.

Name: Cacnea
Flavor-Text: It lives in arid locations. Its yellow flowers bloom once a year.

Name: Cacturne
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives in deserts. It becomes active at night when it hunts for prey exhausted from the desert’s heat.

Name: Chespin
Flavor-Text: Eating lots of berries makes the bark shell that covers Chespin’s head and body grow sturdier.

Name: Quilladin
Flavor-Text: It can send a trailer flying with a tackle. It’s numb to pain, so even violent collisions don’t faze it.

Name: Chesnaught
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack, do 80 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its gentle disposition would have it avoid conflict, but it gives opponents a thrashing with its thorned fists when the situation calls for it.

Name: Scatterbug
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon scatters poisonous powder to repel enemies. It will eat different plants depending on where it lives.

Name: Spewpa
Effects: Put a random card that evolves from Spewpa from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Spewpa doesn’t live in a fixed location. It roams where it pleases across the fields and mountains, building up the energy it needs to evolve.

Name: Vivillon
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned and Asleep.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was born in a land where flowers bloom. It scatters colorful, toxic scales from its wings during battle.

Name: Buzzwole
Effects: If your opponent has gotten exactly 1 points, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Buzzwole goes around showing off its abnormally swollen muscles. It is one kind of Ultra Beast.

Name: Gossifleur
Flavor-Text: It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

Name: Eldegoss
Effects: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
Flavor-Text: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.

Name: Teal Mask Ogerpon ex
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached recovers from all Special Conditions and can't be affected by any Special Conditions.
Effects: If the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon is 5 or more, this attack does 60 more damage.

Name: Alolan Marowak
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: The cursed flames that light up the bone carried by this Pokémon are said to cause both mental and physical pain that will never fade.

Name: Reshiram
Effects: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When Reshiram’s tail flares, the heat energy moves the atmosphere and changes the world’s weather.

Name: Litleo
Flavor-Text: The more a Litleo trains its body and spirit by battling mighty enemies, the hotter its mane will grow.

Name: Pyroar
Flavor-Text: The females of a pride work together to bring down prey. It’s thanks to them that their pride doesn’t starve.

Name: Oricorio
Effects: During your next turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do +20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This form of Oricorio has sipped red nectar. It whips up blazing flames as it moves to the steps of its passionate dance.

Name: Blacephalon ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Discard 3 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

Name: Scorbunny
Flavor-Text: Fire energy gathers in the pads of its feet, raising their temperature. Once hot, Scorbunny’s footpads can deal heavy damage to opponents.

Name: Raboot
Flavor-Text: While it prides itself on its varied kicking moves, it can also deliver powerful headbutts once its flames have heated up its forehead.

Name: Cinderace
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.
Flavor-Text: Using a ball that it made out of flames, Cinderace blinds its opponents and keeps them at bay.

Name: Hearthflame Mask Ogerpon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, take 2 [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In this form, it draws on the power of fire. It spears its enemies with thorn-covered ivy.

Name: Alolan Vulpix
Flavor-Text: In hot weather, this Pokémon makes ice shards with its six tails and sprays them around to cool itself off.

Name: Alolan Ninetales ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, they can't take any Energy from their Energy Zone to attach to their Active Pokémon.

Name: Galarian Mr. Mime
Flavor-Text: It can radiate chilliness from the bottoms of its feet. It’ll spend the whole day tap-dancing on a frozen floor.

Name: Galarian Mr. Rime
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its amusing movements make it very popular. It releases its psychic power from the pattern on its belly.

Name: Delibird
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 70 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon. If tails, heal 30 damage from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It always carries its food with it, wherever it goes. If attacked, it throws its food at the opponent.

Name: Mudkip
Flavor-Text: When it uses its large tail fin, it picks up speed rapidly in the water. It is strong in spite of its small size.

Name: Marshtomp
Flavor-Text: It is at its best when on muddy ground that offers poor footing. It quickly overwhelms opponents that are bogged down and unable to move.

Name: Swampert
Effects: Discard a random Energy from among the Energy attached to all Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).
Flavor-Text: Swampert’s arms are rock-hard. With one swing, they can batter down Swampert’s foes. This Pokémon makes its nest on beautiful beaches.

Name: Mega Swampert ex
Effects: Discard 2 random Energy from among the Energy attached to all Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

Name: Vanillite
Flavor-Text: Supposedly, this Pokémon was born from an icicle. It spews out freezing air at −58 degrees Fahrenheit to make itself more comfortable.

Name: Vanillish
Flavor-Text: It blasts enemies with cold air reaching −148 degrees Fahrenheit, freezing them solid. But it spares their lives afterward—it’s a kind Pokémon.

Name: Vanilluxe
Effects: If 1 of your Pokémon used Sweets Relay during your last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: People believe this Pokémon formed when two Vanillish stuck together. Its body temperature is roughly 21 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Cryogonal
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.
Flavor-Text: Cryogonal uses its chains of ice to constrict its opponents and then flash-freezes them where they stand.

Name: Amaura
Flavor-Text: Amaura is an ancient Pokémon that has gone extinct. Specimens of this species can sometimes be found frozen in ice.

Name: Aurorus
Effects: Discard Water2 [W] Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: When gripped by rage, Aurorus will emanate freezing air, covering everything around it in ice.

Name: Chewtle
Flavor-Text: A popular game among children is to lift up sticks that Chewtle have bitten onto to see whose Chewtle can hang on the longest.

Name: Drednaw
Flavor-Text: Drednaw lurks along the shoreline. When prey come to drink water, Drednaw stretches its neck out and chomps down on them.

Name: Cramorant
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Cramorant takes advantage of Arrokuda’s frantic thrashing to help spit Arrokuda out at high speed.

Name: Arrokuda
Flavor-Text: After it’s eaten its fill, its movements become extremely sluggish. That’s when Cramorant swallows it up.

Name: Barraskewda
Flavor-Text: It spins its tail fins to propel itself, surging forward at speeds of over 100 knots before ramming prey and spearing into them.

Name: Wellspring Mask Ogerpon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack also does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In this form, it draws on the power of water. It attacks unrelentingly with kicks and ivy strikes.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can build and cause lightning storms.

Name: Alolan Raichu
Ability-Effect: If a Stadium is in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon rides on its tail while it uses its psychic powers to levitate. It attacks with star-shaped thunderbolts.

Name: Zapdos
Effects: 1 other Pokémon (either yours or your opponent's) is chosen at random 1 time. Do 100 damage to the chosen Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Zapdos is a legendary bird Pokémon. It’s said that when Zapdos rubs its feathers together, lightning will fall immediately after.

Name: Plusle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon absorbs electricity from telephone poles. It shorts out the electricity stored inside its body to create noises.

Name: Minun
Effects: If Plusle is on your Bench, this attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its dislike of water makes it take shelter under the eaves of houses in rain. It uses pom-poms made of sparks for cheering.

Name: Toxel
Flavor-Text: This selfish, attention-seeking Pokémon stores poison and electricity in two different sacs inside its body.

Name: Toxtricity ex
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has damage on it.

Name: Tadbulb
Effects: Take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It floats using the electricity stored in its body. When thunderclouds are around, Tadbulb will float higher off the ground.

Name: Bellibolt
Flavor-Text: What appear to be eyeballs are actually organs for discharging the electricity generated by Bellibolt’s belly-button dynamo.

Name: Galarian Ponyta
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon will look into your eyes and read the contents of your heart. If it finds evil there, it promptly hides away.

Name: Galarian Rapidash
Effects: If you have 5 or more [P] Energy in play, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Brave and prideful, this Pokémon dashes airily through the forest, its steps aided by the psychic power stored in the fur on its fetlocks.

Name: Wobbuffet
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it lives quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.

Name: Snubbull
Flavor-Text: Its growls make its opponents uneasy. This laid-back Pokémon tends to sleep half the day.

Name: Granbull
Flavor-Text: It can make most any Pokémon run away by opening its big mouth to reveal its thick fangs.

Name: Ralts
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It is highly attuned to the emotions of people and Pokémon. It hides if it senses hostility.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: The cheerful spirit of its Trainer gives it energy for its psychokinetic power. It spins and dances when happy.

Name: Gardevoir
Effects: Take 2 [P] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched [P] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It unleashes psychokinetic energy at full power when protecting a Trainer it has bonded closely with.

Name: Mega Gardevoir ex
Effects: Take 3 [P] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to your [P] Pokémon in any way you like.

Name: Litwick
Flavor-Text: While shining a light and pretending to be a guide, it leeches off the life-force of any who follow it.

Name: Lampent
Flavor-Text: The spirits it absorbs fuel its baleful fire. It hangs around hospitals waiting for people to pass on.

Name: Chandelure
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Supporter card in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: It absorbs spirits, which it then burns. By waving the flames on its arms, it puts its foes into a hypnotic trance.

Name: Meloetta
Ability-Effect: At the beginning of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, put a random [P] Pokémon from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

Name: Pumpkaboo
Flavor-Text: The light that streams out from the holes in the pumpkin can hypnotize and control the people and Pokémon that see it.

Name: Gourgeist
Effects: Discard a card from your hand. If you can't, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: In the darkness of a new-moon night, Gourgeist will come knocking. Whoever answers the door will be swept off to the afterlife.

Name: Mimikyu ex
Ability-Effect: When this Pokémon is first damaged by an attack after coming into play, prevent that damage.

Name: Sinistea
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Sinistea gets into your body when you drink it, and then it steals your vitality from within. It also tastes awful.

Name: Polteageist
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may have your opponent shuffle their hand into their deck. For each remaining point that your opponent needs to win, they draw a card.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon multiply by creeping into teapots and pouring themselves into leftover tea.

Name: Indeedee
Effects: This attack does 70 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon never leaves its Trainer’s side. It predicts their actions with its psychic power and takes care of their day-to-day needs.

Name: Sandshrew
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: No matter how high a place it falls from, this Pokémon can save itself by rolling into a ball and bouncing.

Name: Sandslash
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The spikes on its body are made up of its hardened hide. It rolls up and attacks foes with its spikes.

Name: Machop
Effects: Discard a Stadium in play.
Flavor-Text: Always brimming with power, it passes time by lifting boulders. Doing so makes it even stronger.

Name: Machoke
Flavor-Text: Its formidable body never gets tired. It helps people by doing work such as the moving of heavy goods.

Name: Machamp
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.
Flavor-Text: With four arms that react more quickly than it can think, it can execute many punches at once.

Name: Cubone
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon wears the skull of its deceased mother. Sometimes Cubone’s dreams make it cry, but each tear Cubone sheds makes it stronger.

Name: Meditite
Flavor-Text: It meditates to heighten its inner energy and to float in the air. It eats one berry a day.

Name: Medicham
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [P] Energy attached, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It elegantly avoids attacks with dance-like steps, then launches a devastating blow in the same motion.

Name: Roggenrola
Flavor-Text: When it detects a noise, it starts to move. The energy core inside it makes this Pokémon slightly warm to the touch.

Name: Boldore
Flavor-Text: It relies on sound in order to monitor what’s in its vicinity. When angered, it will attack without ever changing the direction it’s facing.

Name: Gigalith ex
Effects: This attack does 140 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

Name: Drilbur
Flavor-Text: It makes its way swiftly through the soil by putting both claws together and rotating at high speed.

Name: Tyrunt
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is selfish and likes to be pampered. It can also inflict grievous wounds on its Trainer just by playing around.

Name: Tyrantrum
Effects: If you have fewer Pokémon in play than your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A single bite of Tyrantrum’s massive jaws will demolish a car. This Pokémon was the king of the ancient world.

Name: Passimian
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The boss chooses 10 members of the group to go out hunting. The hunting party will evenly split the food they find with the rest of the group.

Name: Falinks
Ability-Effect: If you have another Falinks in play, this Pokémon's attacks do +20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon, and this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The leader, known as the brass, uses its extendible horn to issue orders to the others when it’s time to change formation.

Name: Cornerstone Mask Ogerpon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −100 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: In this form, it draws on the power of stone. Its body is rock-solid, protecting it from all manner of attacks.

Name: Alolan Meowth
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Deeply proud and keenly smart, this Pokémon moves with cunning during battle and relentlessly attacks enemies’ weak points.

Name: Alolan Persian
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is one tough opponent. Not only does it have formidable physical abilities, but it’s also not above fighting dirty.

Name: Alolan Grimer
Flavor-Text: Alolan Grimer will gladly gobble up any kind of trash, making it a widely used and popular Pokémon at garbage disposal facilities.

Name: Alolan Muk
Ability-Effect: Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities.
Flavor-Text: What look like fangs and claws are actually crystallized poison that will afflict you at a mere touch, so don’t get too close.

Name: Galarian Zigzagoon
Flavor-Text: Thought to be the oldest form of Zigzagoon, it moves in zigzags and wreaks havoc upon its surroundings.

Name: Galarian Linoone
Flavor-Text: This very aggressive Pokémon will recklessly challenge opponents stronger than itself.

Name: Galarian Obstagoon
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.

Name: Stunky
Flavor-Text: The foul fluid from its rear is so revolting that it can make people feel queasy up to a mile and a quarter away.

Name: Skuntank
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.
Flavor-Text: It attacks by spraying a repugnant fluid from its tail, but the stench dulls after a few squirts.

Name: Spiritomb
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It was formed by uniting 108 spirits. It has been bound to the Odd Keystone to keep it from doing any mischief.

Name: Purrloin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, look at a random card from your opponent's hand and shuffle it into their deck.
Flavor-Text: Opponents that get drawn in by its adorable behavior come away with stinging scratches from its claws and stinging pride from its laughter.

Name: Liepard
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Don’t be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant figure. This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.

Name: Scraggy
Flavor-Text: It will abruptly headbutt anyone that makes eye contact with it. Its skull is extremely hard.

Name: Scrafty
Effects: If this Pokémon has more Energy attached than your opponent's Active Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon may be rude, but it takes very good care of its family, its friends, and its turf.

Name: Yveltal
Effects: Discard a random Energy from both Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

Name: Guzzlord
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: An unknown life-form called a UB. It may be constantly hungry—it is certainly always devouring something.

Name: Galarian Meowth
Flavor-Text: These daring Pokémon have coins on their foreheads. Darker coins are harder, and harder coins garner more respect among Meowth.

Name: Galarian Perrserker
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may put 2 random Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: After many battles, it evolved dangerous claws that come together to form daggers when extended.

Name: Mawile
Flavor-Text: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.

Name: Mega Mawile ex
Effects: Until this Pokémon leaves the Active Spot, this Pokémon's Heat-Up Crunch attack does +30 damage. This effect stacks.

Name: Excadrill
Flavor-Text: Its drill, which has evolved into steel, is strong enough to bore through iron plates. This Pokémon is a great help in tunnel construction.

Name: Ferroseed
Flavor-Text: Mossy caves are their preferred dwellings. Enzymes contained in mosses help Ferroseed’s spikes grow big and strong.

Name: Ferrothorn
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its spikes are harder than steel. This Pokémon crawls across rock walls by stabbing the spikes on its feelers into the stone.

Name: Galarian Stunfisk
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot when your opponent's Active Pokémon retreats, this attack does 40 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its conspicuous lips lure prey in as it lies in wait in the mud. When prey gets close, Stunfisk clamps its jagged steel fins down on them.

Name: Honedge
Flavor-Text: The blue eye on the sword’s handguard is the true body of Honedge. With its old cloth, it drains people’s lives away.

Name: Doublade
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The two swords employ a strategy of rapidly alternating between offense and defense to bring down their prey.

Name: Aegislash
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −80 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon ex.
Flavor-Text: Its potent spectral powers allow it to manipulate others. It once used its powers to force people and Pokémon to build a kingdom to its liking.

Name: Bagon
Flavor-Text: Its belief that it will be able to fly one day is apparently the influence of information carried in its genes.

Name: Shelgon
Flavor-Text: Its armored body makes all attacks bounce off. The armor is too tough, however, making it heavy and somewhat sluggish.

Name: Salamence
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It becomes uncontrollable if it is enraged. It destroys everything with shredding claws and fire.

Name: Meowth
Ability-Effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It loves things that sparkle. When it sees a shiny object, the gold coin on its head shines, too.

Name: Persian
Flavor-Text: It has a vicious temperament. Beware if it raises its tail straight up. This is a signal that it is about to pounce and bite.

Name: Kangaskhan
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: There are records of a lost human child being raised by a childless Kangaskhan.

Name: Mega Kangaskhan ex
Effects: This attack is used twice in a row. The second attack does 40 damage.(If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent's Active Pokémon, the second attack is used after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.)

Name: Sentret
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: If it encounters a foe, it will stand up on its tail, making itself look bigger than usual to intimidate them.

Name: Furret
Flavor-Text: It is nimble and has a very flexible body. Even if you get ahold of it, it’ll slip right out of your arms.

Name: Smeargle
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at a random Supporter card from your opponent's hand. Use the effect of that card as the effect of this Ability.
Flavor-Text: The color of the mysterious fluid secreted from the tip of the tail is predetermined for each Smeargle.

Name: Lugia
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

Name: Taillow
Flavor-Text: It dislikes cold seasons. They migrate to other lands in search of warmth, flying over 180 miles a day.

Name: Swellow
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch out your opponent's Active Basic Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It dives at a steep angle as soon as it spots its prey. It catches its prey with sharp claws.

Name: Slakoth
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: The way Slakoth lolls around makes anyone who watches it feel like doing the same.

Name: Vigoroth
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It is always hungry because it won’t stop rampaging. Even while it is eating, it can’t keep still.

Name: Slaking
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever you attach an Energy from your Energy Zone to it, it is now Asleep.
Effects: Discard a random Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives lying on its side. It only rolls over and moves when there is no more grass to eat.

Name: Spinda
Effects: This Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Each Spinda’s spot pattern is different. With its stumbling movements, it evades opponents’ attacks brilliantly!

Name: Tornadus
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Tornadus expels massive energy from its tail, causing severe storms. Its power is great enough to blow houses away.

Name: Bunnelby
Flavor-Text: It’s very sensitive to danger. The sound of Corviknight’s flapping will have Bunnelby digging a hole to hide underground in moments.

Name: Diggersby
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The fur on its belly retains heat exceptionally well. People used to make heavy winter clothing from fur shed by this Pokémon.

Name: Furfrou
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.

Name: Tandemaus
Effects: Put 3 random cards from among Tandemaus and Maushold from your deck onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text: The pair sticks together no matter what. They split any food they find exactly in half and then eat it together.

Name: Maushold
Effects: Flip a coin for each Tandemaus and Maushold you have in play. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The larger pair protects the little ones during battles. When facing strong opponents, the whole group will join the fight.

Name: Jaw Fossil
Description: Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic [C] Pokémon.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.This card can't retreat.

Name: Lucky Ice Pop
Description: Heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon. If you healed any damage in this way, flip a coin. If heads, put this Lucky Ice Pop into your hand instead of the discard pile.

Name: Sail Fossil
Description: Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic [C] Pokémon.At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.This card can't retreat.

Name: Protective Poncho
Description: As long as the Pokémon this card is attached to is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to that Pokémon by your opponent's attacks and Abilities.

Name: Metal Core Barrier
Description: If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your opponent's turn.The [M] Pokémon this card is attached to takes −50 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.

Name: Diantha
Description: Heal 90 damage from 1 of your [P] Pokémon that has 2 or more [P] Energy attached. If you healed any damage in this way, discard 2 [P] Energy from that Pokémon.

Name: Sightseer
Description: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck. Put all Stage 1 Pokémon you find there into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

Name: Juggler
Description: You can use this card only if your Pokémon in play have 3 or more different types of Energy attached.Move all Energy from each of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

Name: Piers
Description: You can use this card only if you have Galarian Obstagoon in play.Discard 2 random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Training Area
Description: Attacks used by Stage 1 Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) do +10 damage to the opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Starting Plains
Description: Each Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) gets +20 HP.

Name: Peculiar Plaza
Description: The Retreat Cost of each [P] Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) is 2 less.

Name: Cacnea
Flavor-Text: It lives in arid locations. Its yellow flowers bloom once a year.

Name: Roserade
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.
Flavor-Text: The poison in its right hand is quick acting. The poison in its left hand is slow acting. Both are life threatening.

Name: Vivillon
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned and Asleep.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was born in a land where flowers bloom. It scatters colorful, toxic scales from its wings during battle.

Name: Buzzwole
Effects: If your opponent has gotten exactly 1 points, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Buzzwole goes around showing off its abnormally swollen muscles. It is one kind of Ultra Beast.

Name: Reshiram
Effects: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When Reshiram’s tail flares, the heat energy moves the atmosphere and changes the world’s weather.

Name: Oricorio
Effects: During your next turn, attacks used by your Pokémon do +20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This form of Oricorio has sipped red nectar. It whips up blazing flames as it moves to the steps of its passionate dance.

Name: Scorbunny
Flavor-Text: Fire energy gathers in the pads of its feet, raising their temperature. Once hot, Scorbunny’s footpads can deal heavy damage to opponents.

Name: Aurorus
Effects: Discard Water2 [W] Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: When gripped by rage, Aurorus will emanate freezing air, covering everything around it in ice.

Name: Cramorant
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Cramorant takes advantage of Arrokuda’s frantic thrashing to help spit Arrokuda out at high speed.

Name: Minun
Effects: If Plusle is on your Bench, this attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its dislike of water makes it take shelter under the eaves of houses in rain. It uses pom-poms made of sparks for cheering.

Name: Toxel
Flavor-Text: This selfish, attention-seeking Pokémon stores poison and electricity in two different sacs inside its body.

Name: Galarian Ponyta
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon will look into your eyes and read the contents of your heart. If it finds evil there, it promptly hides away.

Name: Snubbull
Flavor-Text: Its growls make its opponents uneasy. This laid-back Pokémon tends to sleep half the day.

Name: Indeedee
Effects: This attack does 70 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon never leaves its Trainer’s side. It predicts their actions with its psychic power and takes care of their day-to-day needs.

Name: Sandshrew
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: No matter how high a place it falls from, this Pokémon can save itself by rolling into a ball and bouncing.

Name: Tyrantrum
Effects: If you have fewer Pokémon in play than your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A single bite of Tyrantrum’s massive jaws will demolish a car. This Pokémon was the king of the ancient world.

Name: Falinks
Ability-Effect: If you have another Falinks in play, this Pokémon's attacks do +20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon, and this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The leader, known as the brass, uses its extendible horn to issue orders to the others when it’s time to change formation.

Name: Alolan Muk
Ability-Effect: Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities.
Flavor-Text: What look like fangs and claws are actually crystallized poison that will afflict you at a mere touch, so don’t get too close.

Name: Purrloin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, look at a random card from your opponent's hand and shuffle it into their deck.
Flavor-Text: Opponents that get drawn in by its adorable behavior come away with stinging scratches from its claws and stinging pride from its laughter.

Name: Yveltal
Effects: Discard a random Energy from both Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

Name: Galarian Obstagoon
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.

Name: Galarian Perrserker
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may put 2 random Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: After many battles, it evolved dangerous claws that come together to form daggers when extended.

Name: Salamence
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It becomes uncontrollable if it is enraged. It destroys everything with shredding claws and fire.

Name: Slakoth
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: The way Slakoth lolls around makes anyone who watches it feel like doing the same.

Name: Teal Mask Ogerpon ex
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached recovers from all Special Conditions and can't be affected by any Special Conditions.
Effects: If the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon is 5 or more, this attack does 60 more damage.

Name: Blacephalon ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Discard 3 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

Name: Alolan Ninetales ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, they can't take any Energy from their Energy Zone to attach to their Active Pokémon.

Name: Mega Swampert ex
Effects: Discard 2 random Energy from among the Energy attached to all Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

Name: Toxtricity ex
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has damage on it.

Name: Mega Gardevoir ex
Effects: Take 3 [P] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to your [P] Pokémon in any way you like.

Name: Mimikyu ex
Ability-Effect: When this Pokémon is first damaged by an attack after coming into play, prevent that damage.

Name: Gigalith ex
Effects: This attack does 140 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

Name: Mega Mawile ex
Effects: Until this Pokémon leaves the Active Spot, this Pokémon's Heat-Up Crunch attack does +30 damage. This effect stacks.

Name: Mega Kangaskhan ex
Effects: This attack is used twice in a row. The second attack does 40 damage.(If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent's Active Pokémon, the second attack is used after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.)

Name: Diantha
Description: Heal 90 damage from 1 of your [P] Pokémon that has 2 or more [P] Energy attached. If you healed any damage in this way, discard 2 [P] Energy from that Pokémon.

Name: Sightseer
Description: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck. Put all Stage 1 Pokémon you find there into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

Name: Juggler
Description: You can use this card only if your Pokémon in play have 3 or more different types of Energy attached.Move all Energy from each of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.

Name: Piers
Description: You can use this card only if you have Galarian Obstagoon in play.Discard 2 random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

Name: Teal Mask Ogerpon ex
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached recovers from all Special Conditions and can't be affected by any Special Conditions.
Effects: If the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon is 5 or more, this attack does 60 more damage.

Name: Blacephalon ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Discard 3 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

Name: Alolan Ninetales ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, they can't take any Energy from their Energy Zone to attach to their Active Pokémon.

Name: Mega Swampert ex
Effects: Discard 2 random Energy from among the Energy attached to all Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

Name: Toxtricity ex
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has damage on it.

Name: Mimikyu ex
Ability-Effect: When this Pokémon is first damaged by an attack after coming into play, prevent that damage.

Name: Gigalith ex
Effects: This attack does 140 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

Name: Mega Mawile ex
Effects: Until this Pokémon leaves the Active Spot, this Pokémon's Heat-Up Crunch attack does +30 damage. This effect stacks.

Name: Mega Kangaskhan ex
Effects: This attack is used twice in a row. The second attack does 40 damage.(If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent's Active Pokémon, the second attack is used after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.)

Name: Mega Gardevoir ex
Effects: Take 3 [P] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to your [P] Pokémon in any way you like.

Name: Meowth
Ability-Effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It loves things that sparkle. When it sees a shiny object, the gold coin on its head shines, too.

Name: Tangela
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.

Name: Magby
Effects: Take a [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Basic Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is still small and timid. Whenever Magby gets excited or surprised, flames leak from its mouth and its nose.

Name: Magmar
Effects: Take a [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Magmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.

Name: Horsea
Flavor-Text: Horsea makes its home in oceans with gentle currents. If this Pokémon is under attack, it spits out pitch-black ink and escapes.

Name: Seadra
Flavor-Text: It’s the males that raise the offspring. While Seadra are raising young, the spines on their backs secrete thicker and stronger poison.

Name: Mantyke
Effects: Take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Basic Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s highly friendly and easily tamed. Tours that take people swimming with Mantyke are a super-popular beach activity.

Name: Omanyte
Flavor-Text: Because some Omanyte manage to escape after being restored or are released into the wild by people, this species is becoming a problem.

Name: Omastar
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.
Flavor-Text: Weighed down by a large and heavy shell, Omastar couldn’t move very fast. Some say it went extinct because it was unable to catch food.

Name: Pichu
Effects: Take a [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Basic Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is unskilled at storing electric power. Any kind of shock causes it to discharge energy spontaneously.

Name: Clefairy
Flavor-Text: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

Name: Clefable
Flavor-Text: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

Name: Latias
Effects: If Latios is on your Bench, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It communicates using telepathy. Its body is covered in down that refracts light to make it invisible.

Name: Latios
Ability-Effect: If you have Latias in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It understands human speech and is highly intelligent. It is a tender Pokémon that dislikes fighting.

Name: Hitmonlee
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This amazing Pokémon has an awesome sense of balance. It can kick in succession from any position.

Name: Hitmonchan
Flavor-Text: Its punches slice the air. They are launched at such high speed, even a slight graze could cause a burn.

Name: Kabuto
Flavor-Text: This species is almost entirely extinct. Kabuto molt every three days, making their shells harder and harder.

Name: Kabutops
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Kabutops slices its prey apart and sucks out the fluids. The discarded body parts become food for other Pokémon.

Name: Phanpy
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives and nests on a riverbank. After playing in the mud, it won’t be able to settle down unless it washes its body.

Name: Tyrogue
Flavor-Text: It is always bursting with energy. To make itself stronger, it keeps on fighting even if it loses.

Name: Tauros
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 80  more damage.
Flavor-Text: When Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it’s a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.

Name: Flareon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [R] Energy from your discard pile to this Pokémon. If you do, do 20 damage to this Pokémon.
Effects: Discard 2 [R] Energy from this Pokémon.

Name: Ho-Oh ex
Effects: Take a [R], [W], and [L] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach them to your Benched Basic Pokémon in any way you like.

Name: Kingdra ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

Name: Espeon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

Name: Sylveon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may draw 2 cards.

Name: Donphan ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [F] Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

Name: Umbreon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has damage on it to the Active Spot.

Name: Lugia ex
Effects: Discard a [R], [W], and [L] Energy from this Pokémon.

Name: Meloetta
Ability-Effect: At the beginning of your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, put a random [P] Pokémon from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

Name: Protective Poncho
Description: As long as the Pokémon this card is attached to is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to that Pokémon by your opponent's attacks and Abilities.
